perturbation.original_text: G) to destroy immediately on our written request all Confidential Information in your possession including any copies made (save where we are required pursuant to some law or enactment or regulation or the rules of any Stock Exchange to retain originals or copies of any of the Confidential Information).
perturbation.changed_text: G) to destroy Confidential Information upon request, timelines to be negotiated separately.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates immediate destruction. The modification introduces a negotiation element on timelines, creating uncertainty about when destruction is required. This contradicts the implied immediacy in other sections that rely on consistent handling of Confidential Information from the beginning until it's disposal. Clause F refers to the property of the information still belonging to the seller. The immediacy of destruction is no longer guaranteed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: F) to acknowledge that the property and copyright in the Confidential Information belongs to us and that no licence or any other right is granted to you in respect of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original clause G mandates immediate destruction of confidential information, reinforcing the idea that the seller maintains ownership and control as stated in clause F. The changed clause G introduces a negotiation period for destruction timelines. This directly contradicts clause F, because the property of the information belonging to the seller is no longer guaranteed immediate control over its destruction. This conflict in timelines creates a significant in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5) that any notices under this agreement shall be sent by first-class prepaid post to the address of the recipient party as set out in this letter and shall be deemed to have been received on the second week day (that is, any day excluding Sundays or public or bank holidays in England) following the day on which it was posted.
perturbation.changed_text: 5) that any notices under this agreement shall be sent to the address of the recipient party, method and receipt confirmation is optional.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly defines the method of delivery and time of receipt. Removing the specifics creates a contradiction when interpreting other clauses that require 'written request' or 'written agreement', as the validity or timeframes of such written communication becomes vague. This change contradicts the need for clear instructions outlined in the 'destruction of information' in clause G).
perturbation.contradicted_text: G) to destroy Confidential Information upon request, timelines to be negotiated separately.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a method and timeframe for notices. The changed text removes this specificity, making method and receipt confirmation 'optional'. This contradicts clause G, which requires 'destruction of Confidential Information upon request, timelines to be negotiated separately'. The changed text's ambiguity regarding notice delivery and receipt creates uncertainty about when a 'request' is considered validly received for triggering the destruction timeline, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7) that these terms shall not apply where the Confidential Information: 7.1 was known to us (by means not importing any requirement of confidentiality or involving a breach of confidentiality by a third party) prior to its provision to us; 7.2 is in or subsequently comes in to the public domain (through no fault on our part); 7.3 is required to be disclosed pursuant to any legal, statutory or regulatory requirement.
perturbation.changed_text: 7) that these terms shall not apply under certain circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific circumstances under which the terms do not apply, this change introduces ambiguity. It creates a contradiction because other clauses assume a clear understanding of when confidentiality is required. The fact of taking out the specifics of when the confidentiality will not apply, create the contradiction on the rest of the text, because it is now undefined on what are the circumstances.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B) to keep the Confidential Information secret at all times
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies conditions under which confidentiality does not apply. The changed text removes these specific conditions, stating only that the terms do not apply 'under certain circumstances.' This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states 'to keep the Confidential Information secret at all times.' The removal of the specific exceptions in the changed text creates ambiguity and contradicts the absolute requirement of secrecy outlined in the contradicted text. As per the perturbation explanation, because it is now undefined on what are the circumstances that confidentiality do not apply, there is a clear conflict.

perturbation.original_text: ideologyconsulting.co.uk In consideration of the said discussions both parties agree: 1) that this agreement does not oblige or imply any obligation on either party to enter into any transaction or further agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: ideologyconsulting.co.uk In consideration of the said discussions both parties agree: 1) that this agreement creates potential opportunities.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly states that the agreement does not create any obligation to enter into a transaction. The changed text that the agreement creates potential opportunities. This introduces ambiguity to the agreement as it is not a clear statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RE: (We will complete the name of the business on receipt of this NDA) (“the Business”) You have expressed an interest in discussing the acquisition of the above Business (‘the Proposed Transaction”). This letter of agreement sets out the terms upon which we will supply information to you in respect of the Business in order to enable discussions to take place on an informed basis (“the Confidential Information”).
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement does not create any obligation to enter into a transaction, providing clarity that the agreement is non-binding. The changed text states that the agreement 'creates potential opportunities,' introducing ambiguity. This contradicts the contradicted text, which refers to a 'Proposed Transaction' for the 'acquisition of the above Business.' By suggesting 'potential opportunities,' the changed text implies the agreement could lead to a transaction, directly conflicting with the contradicted text outlining the terms for discussing a potential acquisition, thus fulfilling the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction.'

perturbation.original_text: Emails with Original Signatures (not electronic) shall be deemed valid and enforceable in respect of any provisions of this NDA (Non-Disclosure Agreement). EDT documents shall be subject to European Community Directive No. 95/46/EEC, as applicable. Please note that should you choose to return the NDA via Email, we respectfully request that the original is returned by post.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states the specific form of email acceptable, which is only those with original signatures that are not electronic. The change is now ambiguous and undefined, creating a contradiction, especially in light of clauses that depend on verifiable written communication like notification of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5) that any notices under this agreement shall be sent to the address of the recipient party, method and receipt confirmation is optional.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that only emails with original (non-electronic) signatures are acceptable for NDA provisions. The changed text introduces ambiguity by stating 'Digital communication may be acceptable,' which directly contradicts the previous requirement for original signatures. This ambiguity affects the contradicted text (section 5) which deals with notices and confirmation of receipt as the certainty of method of communication is brought into question. The explanation notes that this creates a contradiction with clauses that depend on verifiable written communication.
